{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 30年), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531年}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013年9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36年}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17年}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30年}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3年}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} （In {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 30年), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531年}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013年9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年}}, is preserved as a treasure. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36年}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17年}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. revision.text:
{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 30年), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531年}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013年9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36年}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17年}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30年}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3年}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27年11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36年}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17年}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30年}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3年}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | {{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 30年), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531年}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013年9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36年}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17年}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30年}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3年}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | {{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 30年), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year }}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36年}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17年}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30年}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year }} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} {{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018年}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 30年), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year }}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36年}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year }}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30年}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year }} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 {{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year }} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 30年), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year }}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36年}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year }}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30年}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year }} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} （In {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year }} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year }}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36年}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year }}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year }}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year }} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 （In {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year }} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year }}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36th year }}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year }}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year }}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year }} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} （In {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year }} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year }}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36th year }}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year }}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year }}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year }} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year }}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year }} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} {{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year }} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year }}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36th year }}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year }}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year }}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year }} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year }} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year }}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36th year }}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year }}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year }}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year }} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipal Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Natsuyama Fire Festival}}

The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|12=draft}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36th year|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日|12=draft}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year|12=draft}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄|12=draft}} and straw crafts. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year|12=draft}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipal Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Natsuyama Fire Festival}}

Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year|12=draft}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年|12=draft}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風|12=draft}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year|12=draft}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipal Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Natsuyama Fire Festival}}

{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り|12=draft}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|12=draft}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町|12=draft}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り|12=draft}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年|12=draft}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|12=draft}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|12=draft}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|12=draft}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36th year|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日|12=draft}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year|12=draft}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄|12=draft}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|12=draft}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year|12=draft}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年|12=draft}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風|12=draft}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year|12=draft}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipal Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Natsuyama Fire Festival}}

The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year|12=draft}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipal Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Natsuyama Fire Festival}}

{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り|12=draft}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|12=draft}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町|12=draft}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り|12=draft}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年|12=draft}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|12=draft}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|12=draft}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|12=draft}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36th year|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日|12=draft}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year|12=draft}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄|12=draft}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|12=draft}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year|12=draft}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年|12=draft}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風|12=draft}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year|12=draft}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipal Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Natsuyama Fire Festival}}
