The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|12=draft|12=draft}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|12=draft|12=draft}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|12=draft|12=draft}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36th year|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄|12=draft|12=draft}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|12=draft|12=draft}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年|12=draft|12=draft}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風|12=draft|12=draft}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipal Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Natsuyama Fire Festival}}

{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36th year}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipal Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Natsuyama Fire Festival}}

revision.comment: 24 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り up to rev 97965839
revision.text:
{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記= |種類=[[火祭り]] |画像=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |画像サイズ=250px |画像説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=旧暦9月9日に一番近い土曜日または日曜日
（[[2018年]]（[[平成]]30年）は10月14日） |初回開催= |会場=[[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[夏山町]]字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|火祭りで使用する獅子]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|参詣者を追い回す鬼（左奥）]] '''夏山八幡宮火祭り'''（なつやまはちまんぐう ひまつり）は、[[愛知県]][[岡崎市]]の[[夏山八幡宮]]で行われている[[火祭り]]。 == 概要 == 神社が創建された当時（[[531年]]）から続いている火祭り神事とされ{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }}、ときに「奇祭」と呼ばれる[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013年9月号]。岡崎市の無形[[民俗文化財]]に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }}。 == 歴史 == 火祭りに使用したと言われる[[永禄]]元年（[[1558年]]）銘のある獅子頭一頭が宝物として伝承されている。 火祭りは夏山八幡宮の年4回行われる中祭の一つに位置づけられ、以前は2日間にわたって祭礼が行われた。オタイヤと呼ばれる宵祭りを柿平地区が、本楽（ほんがく）を平針地区が担当していた。柿平地区で新たに鬼面が創られる前は、平針地区の鬼面を借りて祭りを行っていた。現在のように祭りの仕切りを平針と柿平が隔年で交互に行うようになったのは、[[1903年]]（[[明治]]36年）からである{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }}。 [[2005年]]（[[平成]]17年）[[9月16日]]に市の無形民俗文化財に指定された。 == 祭りの内容 == 準備は1週間前の[[注連縄]]作りとわら細工作りに始まる。当日の12時30分頃、氏子は鉈を手に境内に集まり、境内周囲の森から生木を切り出し「ソダ山」を築く。一方、鬼役を務める5人の若者（太夫と呼ばれる）は裸で滝に打たれて身を清める。この「水垢離（みずごり）し潔斎」の場所は担当地区で異なる。平針は不動明王を祀る滝、柿平は八幡宮裏手にある庚申を祀る川の淵である。 水垢離から戻った太夫たちは火きり神事に臨む。矢竹を垂直に立て、両手で錐を揉むようにして途切れないように交代しながら火を熾す。30分～1時間しても採火できないときは、先輩から再び潔斎をするように指示がある。再度、滝で身を清めるが2度目以降は平針も柿平の庚申淵へ行く。 採火ができると合図の太鼓が鳴らされ、ロウソクと火縄に点火。その後、神降ろしと呼ばれる儀式を行う。 神職により御神火がソダ山へ点火されると、太夫らは交代でムシロを使って火を扇ぐ。ソダ山の中央の竹が大音響で破裂するの合図にいよいよ鬼追いの開始となる。雄獅子と雌獅子が噛み合う獅子舞（かましょい）などの所作事ののち、参詣者は「ボケ鬼、ボケ鬼」と囃したて鬼を挑発する。鬼は火のついた木を当てるべく人々を追い回す。鬼役の5人が交代時に相撲、鈴の舞、薙刀の舞、小刀の目利き、獅子打ちの所作を行うのも見所である。相撲に勝利、または見本どおりの所作ができれば交代し、そうでなければ再度、燃え木を手に参詣者を追いかけることになる。 鬼の燃え木に打たれると厄除けとなり、その年は病気にかからないと言われる。 [[2018年]]（[[平成]]30年）は[[10月14日]]に開催された。[[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）は10月12日に開催予定であったが、[[令和元年東日本台風]]（台風19号）の影響で中止。[[2020年]]（令和2年）は不明。[[2021年]]（令和3年）は新型コロナウイルス感染拡大のため中止{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }}。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == 関連項目 == * [[夏山八幡宮]] * [[日本の祭り一覧]] == 外部リンク == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27年11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{デフォルトソート:なつやまはちまんくう ひまつり}} [[Category:岡崎市の祭り]] [[Category:岡崎市の歴史]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]]

{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記= |種類=[[火祭り]] |画像=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |画像サイズ=250px |画像説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=旧暦9月9日に一番近い土曜日または日曜日
（[[2018年]]（[[平成]]30年）は10月14日） |初回開催= |会場=[[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[夏山町]]字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|火祭りで使用する獅子]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|参詣者を追い回す鬼（左奥）]] '''夏山八幡宮火祭り'''（なつやまはちまんぐう ひまつり）は、[[愛知県]][[岡崎市]]の[[夏山八幡宮]]で行われている[[火祭り]]。 == 概要 == 神社が創建された当時（[[531年]]）から続いている火祭り神事とされ{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }}、ときに「奇祭」と呼ばれる[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013年9月号]。岡崎市の無形[[民俗文化財]]に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }}。 == 歴史 == 火祭りに使用したと言われる[[永禄]]元年（[[1558年]]）銘のある獅子頭一頭が宝物として伝承されている。 火祭りは夏山八幡宮の年4回行われる中祭の一つに位置づけられ、以前は2日間にわたって祭礼が行われた。オタイヤと呼ばれる宵祭りを柿平地区が、本楽（ほんがく）を平針地区が担当していた。柿平地区で新たに鬼面が創られる前は、平針地区の鬼面を借りて祭りを行っていた。現在のように祭りの仕切りを平針と柿平が隔年で交互に行うようになったのは、[[1903年]]（[[明治]]36年）からである{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }}。 [[2005年]]（[[平成]]17年）[[9月16日]]に市の無形民俗文化財に指定された。 == 祭りの内容 == 準備は1週間前の[[注連縄]]作りとわら細工作りに始まる。当日の12時30分頃、氏子は鉈を手に境内に集まり、境内周囲の森から生木を切り出し「ソダ山」を築く。一方、鬼役を務める5人の若者（太夫と呼ばれる）は裸で滝に打たれて身を清める。この「水垢離（みずごり）し潔斎」の場所は担当地区で異なる。平針は不動明王を祀る滝、柿平は八幡宮裏手にある庚申を祀る川の淵である。 水垢離から戻った太夫たちは火きり神事に臨む。矢竹を垂直に立て、両手で錐を揉むようにして途切れないように交代しながら火を熾す。30分～1時間しても採火できないときは、先輩から再び潔斎をするように指示がある。再度、滝で身を清めるが2度目以降は平針も柿平の庚申淵へ行く。 採火ができると合図の太鼓が鳴らされ、ロウソクと火縄に点火。その後、神降ろしと呼ばれる儀式を行う。 神職により御神火がソダ山へ点火されると、太夫らは交代でムシロを使って火を扇ぐ。ソダ山の中央の竹が大音響で破裂するの合図にいよいよ鬼追いの開始となる。雄獅子と雌獅子が噛み合う獅子舞（かましょい）などの所作事ののち、参詣者は「ボケ鬼、ボケ鬼」と囃したて鬼を挑発する。鬼は火のついた木を当てるべく人々を追い回す。鬼役の5人が交代時に相撲、鈴の舞、薙刀の舞、小刀の目利き、獅子打ちの所作を行うのも見所である。相撲に勝利、または見本どおりの所作ができれば交代し、そうでなければ再度、燃え木を手に参詣者を追いかけることになる。 鬼の燃え木に打たれると厄除けとなり、その年は病気にかからないと言われる。 [[2018年]]（[[平成]]30年）は[[10月14日]]に開催された。[[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）は10月12日に開催予定であったが、[[令和元年東日本台風]]（台風19号）の影響で中止。[[2020年]]（令和2年）は不明。[[2021年]]（令和3年）は新型コロナウイルス感染拡大のため中止{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }}。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == 関連項目 == * [[夏山八幡宮]] * [[日本の祭り一覧]] == 外部リンク == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27年11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{デフォルトソート:なつやまはちまんくう ひまつり}} [[Category:岡崎市の祭り]] [[Category:岡崎市の歴史]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]]

{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36th year}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipal Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Natsuyama Fire Festival}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り|12=draft}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|12=draft}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町|12=draft}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り|12=draft}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年|12=draft}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|12=draft}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|12=draft}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|12=draft}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36th year|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日|12=draft}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year|12=draft}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄|12=draft}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|12=draft}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|12=draft}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year|12=draft}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年|12=draft}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風|12=draft}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year|12=draft}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipal Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Natsuyama Fire Festival}}

{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り|12=draft}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|12=draft}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町|12=draft}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り|12=draft}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年|12=draft}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|12=draft}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|12=draft}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|12=draft}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36th year|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日|12=draft}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year|12=draft}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄|12=draft}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|12=draft}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|12=draft}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year|12=draft}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年|12=draft}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風|12=draft}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year|12=draft}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Natsuyama Fire Festival}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipal Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]]

{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Festival|qq=}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2018|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki|qq=}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama|qq=}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/531|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk cultural property|qq=}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1558|qq=}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakidaira District|qq=}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru District|qq=}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni masks|qq=}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1903|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 16|qq=}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2005|qq=}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimenawa|qq=}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2018|qq=}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Hagibis|qq=}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|qq=}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipal Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]]

Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala|2=ja|3=不動明王|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2018|qq=}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Hagibis|qq=}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|qq=}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipal Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]]

{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Festival|qq=}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|ja|2018th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2018|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki|qq=}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama|qq=}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/531|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk cultural property|qq=}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1558|qq=}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakidaira District|qq=}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru District|qq=}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni masks|qq=}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1903|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 16|qq=}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2005|qq=}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimenawa|qq=}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala|2=ja|3=不動明王|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2018|qq=}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Hagibis|qq=}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|qq=}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]]

[[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire Festival|qq=}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|ja|531st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/531|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk cultural property|qq=}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1558|qq=}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakidaira District|qq=}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru District|qq=}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni masks|qq=}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1903|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|ja|9月16日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 16|qq=}}, {{ill|2005|ja|平成17年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2005|qq=}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimenawa|qq=}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala|2=ja|3=不動明王|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2018|qq=}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|ja|令和元年東日本台風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon Hagibis|qq=}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|qq=}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]]

The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|qq=}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類=[[:en:Festival of fire|Fire Festival]] |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In [[:en:2018|2018]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]]{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama|qq=}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire Festival]] held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} in [[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in [[:en:531|531]].{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk cultural property|qq=}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1558|qq=}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakidaira District|qq=}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru District|qq=}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni masks|qq=}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1903|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On [[:en:September 16|September 16]], {{ill|2005|ja|平成17年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2005|qq=}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of [[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]] and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2018|qq=}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, the event was canceled due to [[:en:Typhoon Hagibis|Typhoon Hagibis]]. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|qq=}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]]

The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|qq=}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岡崎市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:岡崎市の祭り
revision.text:
{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類=[[:en:Festival of fire|Fire Festival]] |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In [[:en:2018|2018]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]]{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama|qq=}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire Festival]] held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} in [[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in [[:en:531|531]].{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk cultural property|qq=}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1558|qq=}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakidaira District|qq=}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru District|qq=}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni masks|qq=}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1903|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On [[:en:September 16|September 16]], {{ill|2005|ja|平成17年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2005|qq=}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of [[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]] and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2018|qq=}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, the event was canceled due to [[:en:Typhoon Hagibis|Typhoon Hagibis]]. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|qq=}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岡崎市の歴史]] [[Category:岡崎市の祭り]]

However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, the event was canceled due to [[:en:Typhoon Hagibis|Typhoon Hagibis]]. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|qq=}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[en:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岡崎市の歴史]] [[Category:Festivals of Okazaki]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類=[[:en:Festival of fire|Fire Festival]] |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In [[:en:2018|2018]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]]{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama|qq=}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire Festival]] held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} in [[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in [[:en:531|531]].{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk cultural property|qq=}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|ja|永禄元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1558|qq=}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakidaira District|qq=}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru District|qq=}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni masks|qq=}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|ja|明治36年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1903|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On [[:en:September 16|September 16]], {{ill|2005|ja|平成17年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2005|qq=}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of [[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]] and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|qq=}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|ja|平成30年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2018|qq=}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|ja|令和元年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, the event was canceled due to [[:en:Typhoon Hagibis|Typhoon Hagibis]]. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|ja|令和3年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|qq=}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] }} [[sn:Natsuyama Fire Festival]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岡崎市の歴史]] [[Category:Festivals of Okazaki]]
