（In [[:en:2018|2018]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]]{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama|1=Natsuyama-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429972}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire Festival]] held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} in [[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in [[:en:531|531]].{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk cultural property|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from [[:en:1558|1558]], is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakidaira District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni masks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in [[:en:1903|Meiji 36]].{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On [[:en:September 16|September 16]], [[:en:2005|Heisei 17]], the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of [[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]] and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In [[:en:2018|Heisei 30]], the festival was held on October 14. However, in [[:en:2019|2019]], the event was canceled due to [[:en:Typhoon Hagibis|Typhoon Hagibis]]. The festival was also canceled in [[:en:2021|Reiwa 3]] due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|1=list of Japanese festivals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10312376}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] }} [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Festivals of Okazaki]] {{wikidata link|Q30925230}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In [[:en:2018|Heisei 30]], the festival was held on October 14. However, in [[:en:2019|2019]], the event was canceled due to [[:en:Typhoon Hagibis|Typhoon Hagibis]]. The festival was also canceled in [[:en:2021|Reiwa 3]] due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|1=list of Japanese festivals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10312376}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] }} [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Festivals of Okazaki]] {{wikidata link|Q30925230}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30925230]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類=[[:en:Festival of fire|Fire Festival]] |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In [[:en:2018|2018]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]]{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama|1=Natsuyama-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429972}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire Festival]] held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} in [[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in [[:en:531|531]].{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk cultural property|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from [[:en:1558|1558]], is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakidaira District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni masks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in [[:en:1903|Meiji 36]].{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On [[:en:September 16|September 16]], [[:en:2005|Heisei 17]], the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of [[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]] and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In [[:en:2018|Heisei 30]], the festival was held on October 14. However, in [[:en:2019|2019]], the event was canceled due to [[:en:Typhoon Hagibis|Typhoon Hagibis]]. The festival was also canceled in [[:en:2021|Reiwa 3]] due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|1=list of Japanese festivals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10312376}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] }} [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Festivals of Okazaki]] {{wikidata link|Q30925230}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In [[:en:2018|Heisei 30]], the festival was held on October 14. However, in [[:en:2019|2019]], the event was canceled due to [[:en:Typhoon Hagibis|Typhoon Hagibis]]. The festival was also canceled in [[:en:2021|Reiwa 3]] due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|1=list of Japanese festivals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10312376}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] {{wikidata link|Q30925230}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals of Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q30925230]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類=[[:en:Festival of fire|Fire Festival]] |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In [[:en:2018|2018]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]]{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama|1=Natsuyama-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429972}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a [[:en:Festival of fire|Fire Festival]] held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} in [[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in [[:en:531|531]].{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk cultural property|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from [[:en:1558|1558]], is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakidaira District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni masks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in [[:en:1903|Meiji 36]].{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On [[:en:September 16|September 16]], [[:en:2005|Heisei 17]], the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of [[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]] and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In [[:en:2018|Heisei 30]], the festival was held on October 14. However, in [[:en:2019|2019]], the event was canceled due to [[:en:Typhoon Hagibis|Typhoon Hagibis]]. The festival was also canceled in [[:en:2021|Reiwa 3]] due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|1=list of Japanese festivals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10312376}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] {{wikidata link|Q30925230}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals of Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

{{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk cultural property|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|en|1558|WD=Q6601|lt=1558}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakidaira District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni masks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=Meiji 36}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|en|September 16|WD=Q2897|lt=September 16}}, {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=Heisei 17}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|en|Shimenawa|WD=Q1887421|lt=Shimenawa}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=Heisei 30}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|en|Typhoon Hagibis|WD=Q69841946|lt=Typhoon Hagibis}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=Reiwa 3}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|1=list of Japanese festivals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10312376}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] {{wikidata link|Q30925230}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals of Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} in {{ill|Okazaki, Aichi|en|Okazaki, Aichi|WD=Q242783|lt=Okazaki}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|en|531|WD=Q32871|lt=531}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk cultural property|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used revision.text:
[[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] {{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire Festival}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|Okazaki, Aichi|en|Okazaki, Aichi|WD=Q242783|lt=Okazaki}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama|1=Natsuyama-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429972}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire Festival}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} in {{ill|Okazaki, Aichi|en|Okazaki, Aichi|WD=Q242783|lt=Okazaki}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|en|531|WD=Q32871|lt=531}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk cultural property|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|en|1558|WD=Q6601|lt=1558}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakidaira District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni masks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=Meiji 36}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|en|September 16|WD=Q2897|lt=September 16}}, {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=Heisei 17}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|en|Shimenawa|WD=Q1887421|lt=Shimenawa}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=Heisei 30}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|en|Typhoon Hagibis|WD=Q69841946|lt=Typhoon Hagibis}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=Reiwa 3}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachimangu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|1=list of Japanese festivals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10312376}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] {{wikidata link|Q30925230}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals of Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|en|1558|WD=Q6601|lt=1558|1=1558}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakidaira District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni masks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=Meiji 36|1=1903}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|en|September 16|WD=Q2897|lt=September 16|1=September 16}}, {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=Heisei 17|1=2005}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|en|Shimenawa|WD=Q1887421|lt=Shimenawa|1=Shimenawa}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=Heisei 30|1=2018}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019|1=2019}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|en|Typhoon Hagibis|WD=Q69841946|lt=Typhoon Hagibis|1=Typhoon Hagibis}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=Reiwa 3|1=2021}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|1=List of festivals in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10312376}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] {{wikidata link|Q30925230}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals of Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

In {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=Heisei 30|1=2018}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019|1=2019}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|en|Typhoon Hagibis|WD=Q69841946|lt=Typhoon Hagibis|1=Typhoon Hagibis}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=Reiwa 3|1=2021}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|1=List of festivals in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10312376}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:夏山八幡宮火祭り]] {{wikidata link|Q30925230}} [[Category:Festivals of Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=夏山八幡宮火祭り |英文表記=Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival |種類={{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire Festival|1=Fire Festival}} |image=Natsuyama-Hachimangu-1.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明= |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Old lunar calendar: Closest Saturday or Sunday to September 9
（In {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 30th year ), held on October 14） |初回開催= |会場={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|Okazaki, Aichi|en|Okazaki, Aichi|WD=Q242783|lt=Okazaki|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}{{ill|Natsuyama|ja|夏山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama|1=Natsuyama-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429972}}字宮本 |主催= |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス= |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Lion used during the festival]] [[File:Natsuyama-Hachimangu-6.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Oni chasing worshippers]] '''Natsuyama Hachimangu Fire Festival''' (夏山八幡宮火祭り) is a {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Fire Festival|1=Fire Festival}} held at {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} in {{ill|Okazaki, Aichi|en|Okazaki, Aichi|WD=Q242783|lt=Okazaki|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. == Overview == This fire festival has been held since the shrine's founding in {{ill|531|en|531|WD=Q32871|lt=531|1=531}}.{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/44natsuyama.pdf | title= 岡崎まちものがたり 44 夏山学区 | publisher= 岡崎市 市制100周年記念サイト |format=PDF |date= | accessdate = 2020-7-23 }} It is sometimes referred to as a "peculiar festival."[http://www.okazaki-lita.com/uploads/05/05/001/13/05-05-001-13-09.pdf 岡崎市東部地域交流センター 情報誌 「むらさきかん」2013rd year 9月号] The festival is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk cultural property|ja|民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk cultural property|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Okazaki City.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }} == History == A lion head used during the festival, engraved with a date from {{ill|1558|en|1558|WD=Q6601|lt=1558|1=1558}}, is preserved as a treasure. The fire festival is one of the four mid-sized annual festivals held at Natsuyama Hachimangu. Previously, the festival spanned two days, with the evening ceremony (known as "Otaiya") managed by the {{ill|Kakidaira District|ja|柿平地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakidaira District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while the main ceremony ("Hongaku") was overseen by the {{ill|Hirabaru District|ja|平針地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Before Kakidaira created their own {{ill|Oni masks|ja|鬼面|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni masks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, they borrowed masks from Hirabaru. The current alternating management of the festival between Hirabaru and Kakidaira began in {{ill|1903|en|1903|WD=Q2045|lt=Meiji 36|1=1903}}.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou7.pdf |title=岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画 - 第2章 額田地区の山里のくらしにみる歴史的風致 |format=PDF |publisher=[[岡崎市役所]] |accessdate=2018-4-14 }} On {{ill|September 16|en|September 16|WD=Q2897|lt=September 16|1=September 16}}, {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=Heisei 17|1=2005}}, the festival was designated an intangible folk cultural property by the city. == Festival Details == Preparations begin a week in advance with the creation of {{ill|Shimenawa|en|Shimenawa|WD=Q1887421|lt=Shimenawa|1=Shimenawa}} and straw crafts. On the festival day, around 12:30 PM, parishioners gather with hatchets to cut fresh wood from the forest around the shrine grounds, constructing a structure called "Soda-yama." Meanwhile, five young men, called "Daifu," serving as Oni, cleanse themselves under a waterfall while naked. The cleansing ritual location differs by district: Hirabaru uses a waterfall dedicated to {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}, while Kakidaira uses a river behind the shrine dedicated to {{ill|Kōshin|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. After purification, the Daifu perform the sacred fire-starting ritual, rubbing a bamboo drill against another bamboo piece to generate fire. If unsuccessful within 30 minutes to an hour, senior members instruct another round of purification. On subsequent attempts, all participants use Kakidaira's Kōshin river regardless of district. Once fire is successfully kindled, a drum signals the lighting of candles and torches, followed by a ritual known as "Kami-oroshi" (God Descending). When the sacred fire ignites the Soda-yama, the Daifu fan the flames with straw mats. At the sound of an explosive bamboo crack, the Oni begin chasing worshippers. After a lion dance called "Kamashoi," worshippers provoke the Oni with chants of "Boke Oni, Boke Oni." The Oni respond by pursuing people with burning sticks. Victory in rituals such as sumo, sword dance, or precise maneuvers allows Daifu to switch roles, otherwise, they continue chasing participants. Being struck by the Oni’s burning stick is believed to ward off misfortune and prevent illness for the year. In {{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=Heisei 30|1=2018}}, the festival was held on October 14. However, in {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019|1=2019}}, the event was canceled due to {{ill|Typhoon Hagibis|en|Typhoon Hagibis|WD=Q69841946|lt=Typhoon Hagibis|1=Typhoon Hagibis}}. The festival was also canceled in {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=Reiwa 3|1=2021}} due to the COVID-19 pandemic.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/682 | title=夏山八幡宮火まつり |website=岡崎おでかけナビ |publisher=岡崎市観光協会 |date= | accessdate=2022-1-5 }} == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2011-3-31 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 額田資料編Ⅲ 民俗 |publisher = [[岡崎市役所]] |page = 38-49 |isbn = }} == See also == * {{ill|Natsuyama Hachimangu|ja|夏山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuyama Hachimangu|1=Natsuyama Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429969}} * {{ill|List of Japanese Festivals|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|1=List of festivals in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10312376}} == External Links == * {{YouTube|67dRshzyIZY|夏山八幡宮　火祭り（チャンネルおかざき　平成27th year 11月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} * {{地域文化資産ポータル2|from=14|ContentID=203|date=20121014224059}} {{translated page|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り|version=97965839|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q30925230|ja|夏山八幡宮火祭り}} [[Category:Festivals of Okazaki]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
